title: mecardonia procumbens var. flagellaris
intro: Mecardonia procumbens var. flagellaris, also known as creeping saturday night, is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Scrophulariaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its diuretic and antispasmodic effects, which can help relieve symptoms of urinary tract infections and menstrual cramps. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, providing potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Mecardonia procumbens var. flagellaris can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, making it a versatile herbal remedy.
